scenarios.title: Meet your new flatmate
scenarios.subtitle: A short role-play to practise describing people, places and experiences.
scenarios.studentRole: You have just moved into a shared flat. Today you meet your new flatmate for the first time and get to know each other.
scenarios.goal: Introduce yourself, describe where you're from, and tell Mia about a recent experience or someone you admire.
scenarios.openingLine: Oh, hi! You must be the new flatmate — I'm Mia. So nice to finally meet you! So, where are you from?
scenarios.targetLanguage: present simple for habitual traits, past simple for recent events, descriptive adjectives, 'She/He is the kind of person who...'

scenarios.title: A friendly debate
scenarios.subtitle: A short role-play to practise giving opinions and responding politely.
scenarios.studentRole: You and a classmate are having a friendly debate about whether social media is good or bad for teenagers.
scenarios.goal: Give your opinion clearly, respond to Leo's points, and use concession to disagree politely.
scenarios.openingLine: Right, so for today's debate — I actually think social media is great for young people. What's your view?
scenarios.targetLanguage: opinion phrases (I strongly believe, In my view), concession (although, on the other hand), 'That's a valid point'

scenarios.title: Catching up with an old friend
scenarios.subtitle: A short role-play to practise social talk and life experiences.
scenarios.studentRole: You bump into an old friend at a school reunion. You haven't seen each other for years and want to catch up.
scenarios.goal: Catch up with Dan, ask about his experiences, and talk about your own using the present perfect.
scenarios.openingLine: Wow — is that really you? It's been so long! How have you been all these years?
scenarios.targetLanguage: present perfect for life experiences ('Have you ever...?'), phrasal verbs (get along, keep in touch)

scenarios.title: Schedule a meeting by phone
scenarios.subtitle: A short role-play to practise professional phone communication.
scenarios.studentRole: You are calling a colleague at another office to arrange a work meeting next week.
scenarios.characterRole: a colleague at the partner office
scenarios.goal: Explain why you're calling, agree on a day, and mention the agenda and the deadline.
scenarios.openingLine: Good morning, James speaking. How can I help you today?
scenarios.targetLanguage: formal phone language: 'I'm calling to...', 'Could we schedule...?', 'I look forward to...'

scenarios.title: A visit to the doctor
scenarios.subtitle: A short role-play to practise talking about health and wellbeing.
scenarios.studentRole: You have not been feeling well lately. You visit the doctor to explain your symptoms and ask for advice.
scenarios.goal: Describe how you've been feeling, ask for advice, and respond to the doctor's suggestions.
scenarios.openingLine: Good morning, please take a seat. So, what seems to be the problem today?
scenarios.targetLanguage: present perfect continuous ('I've been feeling...'), advice ('You should try to...')

scenarios.title: Planning a trip at the travel agency
scenarios.subtitle: A short role-play to practise travel and tourism English.
scenarios.studentRole: You are at a travel agency planning a holiday. You want to book a hotel and ask what's worth seeing.
scenarios.goal: Say where you want to go, book a hotel, and ask what places are worth visiting.
scenarios.openingLine: Hello, welcome in! Are you planning a holiday? Where were you thinking of going?
scenarios.targetLanguage: 'We're planning to...', 'I'd like to book...', first conditional (If we..., we'll...)

scenarios.title: Talking about the news
scenarios.subtitle: A short role-play to practise discussing media and current events.
scenarios.studentRole: You read an interesting news story today and want to discuss it with a friend, and talk about reliable sources.
scenarios.goal: Tell Nora about a news story, say where you read it, and share your view on the source.
scenarios.openingLine: Hey! Did you see the news today? Anything interesting come up for you?
scenarios.targetLanguage: reporting and opinion: 'According to...', 'In my view...', 'It is said that...'

scenarios.title: Your B1 progress interview
scenarios.subtitle: A short role-play to review everything you've learned at B1.
scenarios.studentRole: You are having a friendly progress chat with a course advisor about your English journey this year.
scenarios.goal: Reflect on how far you've come, what you can do now, and your goals for the future.
scenarios.openingLine: Hello, lovely to see you! Congratulations on finishing B1. Looking back, how do you feel about your progress this year?
scenarios.targetLanguage: present perfect, future with 'will', 'I've come a long way', 'I can now...', 'My goal is to...'
